---
title: "LTNC wildlife cameras"
subtitle: "Nine-year biodiversity synthesis for two Napa County preserves"
description: "Presentation summarizing long-term mammal community trends, seasonal patterns, and wildlife responses to a large wildfire — built on a fully reproducible R pipeline"
date: "2024-12-15"
image: thumbnail-ltnc-cameras-2024.png
categories: [cameras, biodiversity baseline, mammal monitoring, data synthesis, presentation]
format: html
---

<a class="button-teal" href="https://github.com/morethangray/ltnc_cameras">Repository</a>

### Project overview

This project synthesized nine years of wildlife camera data from Duff Ranch and Dunn-Wildlake Preserve for The Land Trust of Napa County. The goal was to characterize the mammal community over time and evaluate how wildlife responded to a major wildfire event.

### Objectives

- Quantify species richness and relative abundance for target mammal species
- Evaluate camera performance and identify potential monitoring biases
- Identify seasonal and annual trends in the mammal community
- Assess wildlife response to a large wildfire event
- Develop clear visualizations to communicate findings to a non-specialist audience

### Approach

Analysis was conducted in R using a {targets} pipeline for full reproducibility, enabling efficient updates as new data are collected. The pipeline integrates data cleaning, trend analysis, and figure generation in a single documented workflow.

### Key findings

- A diverse wildlife community with distinct seasonal activity patterns
- Species-specific responses to the wildfire, with some taxa showing clear post-fire shifts in detection rates
- Camera performance evaluation identified periods and locations with reduced reliability

### Citation

Gray M. 2024. Tracking wildlife health: Mammal populations at Duff Ranch and Dunn-Wildlake Preserve. A presentation prepared by Pisaster for The Land Trust of Napa County.
